corpus.title: Rahul Gandhi Meets Injured CJP Protesters, Demands PM Modi, Amit Shah's Resignation
corpus.signal_text:
Rahul Gandhi, accompanied by Congress MPs Priyanka Gandhi Vadra and KC Venugopal, visited students injured during police action on the Cockroach Janata Party's (CJP) 'Chalo Sansad' march.
Rahul Gandhi Meets Injured CJP Protesters, Demands PM Modi, Amit Shah's Resignation
Rahul Gandhi also accused the RSS of influencing the education system, alleging that individuals affiliated with the organisation were being appointed as vice-chancellors.
- Rahul Gandhi visited injured students at RML hospital.
- He questioned PM's silence, demanding top ministers' resignations.
- Gandhi alleged Ambani, Adani, RSS influence on education system.
- He stated youth frustration stems from lack of future opportunities.
Leader of Opposition in the Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi on Tuesday visited Ram Manohar Lohia (RML) Hospital to meet protesters injured during the police action on the Cockroach Janata Party's (CJP) 'Chalo Sansad' march. He was accompanied by Congress MPs Priyanka Gandhi Vadra and KC Venugopal.
He questioned PM Modi's silence over the police action against protesters and demanded the resignation of the Prime Minister, Home Minister Amit Shah, and Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan.
Before meeting the injured students, Gandhi said the issue extended far beyond the education system and reflected the growing frustration among young people over their future.
"The point is this is no way to treat the young people of this country. The young people of this country do not have opportunities; all the doors are closed. One door is open for competitive exams, and that has also been destroyed," he said.
ALSO READ: Don't Drag The Court: Delhi HC Refuses Urgent Hearing On Police Action During CJP Protest
'PM Modi, Shah, Pradhan Should Resign'
He alleged that students were protesting not only against alleged irregularities in the examination system but also because they saw no future for themselves.
"These students are complaining not just about the education system but about their future. This is much bigger than education. This is students of India saying they have no future in India. This is legitimate," Gandhi said.
The Congress leader also accused industrialists Mukesh Ambani and Gautam Adani, along with the RSS, of influencing the education system.
"They watch Ambani's wedding and Rs 1,000 crore being spent there, while they have no money to start a business of their own. They are complaining because of Mr Ambani, Mr Adani and the RSS's complete takeover of the educat

corpus.title: Did govt and Delhi Police misread scale of Cockroach Janta Party protest?
corpus.signal_text:
Did govt and Delhi Police misread scale of Cockroach Janta Party protest?
Despite weeks of prior signals, the Delhi Police and the government seem to have failed to accurately estimate the number of protesters who would gather for the march to Parliament on Monday. As the crowd swelled and reached the Parliament doorsteps, the police were clearly caught off guard.
Over the past 15 years, Delhi has witnessed some of the most defining public protests. Be it the 2021 tractor rally, where protesting farmers breached security at the Red Fort, or the 2012 Nirbhaya agitation, where ordinary citizens reached the doors of Raisina Hill. Like the previous incidents, the Delhi Police seems to have misjudged the scale of Monday's Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) protest, which turned violent as thousands of youth clashed with police, who were clearly outnumbered, leaving nearly 200 injured.
That the 'Sansad Chalo' march, demanding reforms in the education system, would take place on July 20 was known. The CJP agitation was on for over a month. Yet, tens of thousands of protesters were able to reach the doorsteps of Parliament before they were lathi-charged and tear-gassed. Around 60 protesters were injured. The injury count on the police side was double the number (118). There were telltale signs that the police failed to accurately estimate how many protesters would take part in Monday's march.
HOW THE COCKROACH PROTESTS UNFOLDED?
The call for the march to Parliament was issued around three weeks ago by the CJP, a satirical movement founded by Abhijeet Dipke. On June 28, activist Sonam Wangchuk started an indefinite hunger strike demanding the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over the NEET-UG paper leak fiasco. In the initial weeks, the protest failed to gather any steam.
The first sign of government intervention was seen on July 18, when Wangchuk was removed by cops and shifted to Safdarjung Hospital in dramatic early-morning scenes. However, instead of ending the agitation, it gave the protest a fresh impetus.
In fact, hundreds more arrived at the Jantar Mantar protest site in solidarity. By Sunday evening, the numbers swelled from 3,000 to around 5,000. According to a report in The Indian Express, the Delhi Police had intel that the turnout would be around 7,000-8,000. However, on Monday, nearly a lakh protesters gathered on the roads leading to Sansad Marg.
Delhi Police said the CJP neither sought permission nor received any nod to hold a marc

corpus.title: India’s ‘cockroach’ movement vows relentless protest until education minister resigns
corpus.signal_text:
- The Cockroach Janta Party pledged to continue nationwide protests until Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan resigns over examination controversies.
- Clashes between protesters and police injured at least 180 people, highlighting escalating tensions over education reform demands.
- The movement has gained significant public support, reflecting broader dissatisfaction with examination integrity and youth employment prospects in India.
NEW DELHI, July 21 — India’s “Cockroach” party movement vowed Tuesday to press ahead with their campaign until the education minister resigns, a day after at least 180 people were injured in protests in New Delhi.
Thousands of protesters gathered in the heart of the Indian capital on Monday, demanding Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation and wider examination reforms, in a show of force by the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) online movement.
The CJP has won millions of followers on social media since its launch in May, fuelling one of the biggest challenges to Hindu-nationalist Prime Minister Narendra Modi since he won his third term in office in 2024.
“We won’t stop. Pradhan has to be sacked,” CJP’s Ashutosh Ranka said in a social media post.
Hundreds of protesters remained camped at the Jantar Mantar protest site on Tuesday, many of them calling for wider changes in the education system.
On Monday, police fired tear gas and baton-charged protesters to stop them for marching on to parliament, in one of the biggest street rallies in Delhi in around five years.
At least 178 people were injured in clashes between protesters and security officials, Delhi Police said.
That included 118 police personnel, including senior officers, it said while criticising what it described as “unruly, aggressive and violent behaviour” by a “violent mob”.
“During the scuffle, around 60 protesters were also reported to get injured,” it said in a statement late Monday.
Protest organisers condemned the “high-handedness” of the authorities and said hundreds of protesters had been injured.
“It’s a shameful day in the history of Indian democracy that people, honest students, who had come for a genuine issue were brutally beaten by the Delhi police,” Ranka told AFP.
Ranka, who met senior minister JP Nadda on Monday, said the government had yet to respond to their demands.
Despite rapid economic growth, millions of people in the world’s most populous nation still struggle to find stable and well-paying jobs, fuelling discontent.

corpus.title: CJP's 'Sansad Chalo': Young woman injured in police action on ventilator
corpus.signal_text:
- News
- City News
- delhi News
- CJP's 'Sansad Chalo': Young woman injured in police action on ventilator at RML Hospital; sit-in enters day 2
Trending
NEW DELHI: A young woman injured during the police action at the Cockroach Janta Party's (CJP) 'Sansad Chalo' protest is on ventilator support at Delhi's Ram Manohar Lohia (RML) Hospital, news agency PTI reported.The woman sustained injuries during Monday's clashes between protesters and police.Congress leaders Rahul Gandhi and Priyanka Gandhi Vadra, AAP chief Arvind Kejriwal, and Union minister JP Nadda visited RML Hospital, where the Opposition leaders met injured protesters while Nadda met doctors treating them.Kejriwal said, "We have been informed that, except for one girl, everyone else has been discharged. All the injured students and all the injured police personnel have been discharged. One girl is in the ICU and is in critical condition."The development comes a day after thousands of CJP supporters attempted to march towards Parliament demanding the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan over alleged examination irregularities.The march witnessed repeated clashes with police after protesters tried to breach barricades despite prohibitory orders under Section 163 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS).

corpus.title: 'Future of youth priority': PM Modi says NEET leak national issue, asks to avoid 'partisan politics'
corpus.signal_text:
'Future of youth priority': PM Modi says NEET leak national issue, asks to avoid 'partisan politics'

More than 170 people, including CJP protesters and Delhi Police personnel, were treated at central government hospitals on Monday following clashes during the 'Chalo Sansad' march, with a young woman admitted to Safdarjung Hospital in critical condition, sources said. According to hospital sources, around 150 injured people were taken to Dr Ram Manohar Lohia (RML) Hospital, while at least 24 others received treatment at Lady Hardinge Medical College (LHMC).
